title: Staying Safe with Digital Payments
concept: Protect your UPI and online payments
summary: UPI and online payments are fast and convenient but attract scammers. A few simple habits, like never sharing your OTP or PIN, keep your money safe.

sections.heading: Never Share OTP or PIN
sections.body: Your UPI PIN and one-time passwords (OTPs) are like the keys to your account, and no genuine bank, app or company will ever ask for them. Anyone asking you to share an OTP or PIN over a call, message or screen-sharing app is trying to defraud you. Treat these as private and never type them at someone else's request.

sections.heading: The Refund and 'Receive Money' Trap
sections.body: A common scam reverses the direction of a payment: you are told you will receive a refund or prize, but you are actually approving money leaving your account. Remember that you never need to enter your UPI PIN to receive money, only to send it. If an app asks for your PIN during a so-called refund, stop immediately.

sections.heading: Verify Before You Pay
sections.body: Before paying, check the receiver's name shown by the app and confirm it matches who you intend to pay. Be wary of fake QR codes stuck over real ones at shops, and of links sent by strangers. When in doubt, pause, verify through an official channel, and never let urgency rush you into paying.

quiz.prompt: Who is allowed to ask you for your UPI PIN or OTP?
options: Anyone calling from an unknown number

quiz.prompt: When do you need to enter your UPI PIN?
options: To send money, not to receive it

quiz.prompt: Someone says you must enter your PIN to 'receive' a refund. What is happening?
options: It is likely a scam to take money from you

quiz.prompt: What should you do before paying via QR code at a shop?
options: Check the receiver's name matches who you intend to pay
Share your PIN with the shopkeeper

quiz.prompt: A stranger sends an urgent payment link. The safest response is to:
options: Pause and verify through an official channel

disclaimer: Educational content only. This is a simulation, not financial advice.
